message:
fix(http): harden CORS default, clamp pagination params, validate order

Three security/robustness fixes:
- Change CORS allow_origins fallback from ["*"] to [] so unconfigured
  CORS does not accidentally allow all origins.
- Clamp limit/page parameters in mail_inbox, unified_inbox, and
  mail_search to [1, 10000] to prevent unbounded queries.
- Validate the order parameter in mail_project and mail_search to only
  accept "relevance", "time", or None, preventing SQL injection via the
  ORDER BY clause.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

         app_any2 = _cast(_Any, fastapi_app)
         app_any2.add_middleware(
             CORSMiddleware,
-            allow_origins=settings.cors.origins or ["*"],
+            allow_origins=settings.cors.origins or [],
             allow_credentials=settings.cors.allow_credentials,
             allow_methods=settings.cors.allow_methods or ["*"],
             allow_headers=settings.cors.allow_headers or ["*"],
@@ -1936,6 +1936,8 @@ async def mail_project(
